name: nacls
title: AWS Firewall — Network ACLs
summary: Creates stateless network ACLs with numbered allow/deny rules and optional subnet associations.

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names and mandatory tags.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Applied as the mandatory 'owner' tag.

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code for chargeback/showback. Applied as the mandatory 'cost_center' tag.

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Optional additional tags merged beneath the mandatory tag set. Mandatory tags win on key collision.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: vpc_id
module.description: ID of the VPC in which to create the network ACLs. Supplied by ID — no framework dependency is implied.

module.name: network_acls
module.type: map(object({ subnet_ids = optional(list(string), []) ingress_rules = optional(map(object({ rule_number = number protocol = string action = string cidr_block = optional(string) from_port = optional(number) to_port = optional(number) icmp_type = optional(number) icmp_code = optional(number) })), {}) egress_rules = optional(map(object({ rule_number = number protocol = string action = string cidr_block = optional(string) from_port = optional(number) to_port = optional(number) icmp_type = optional(number) icmp_code = optional(number) })), {}) }))
module.description: Map of network ACLs keyed by logical name. Each ACL carries ingress and egress rule maps and an optional list of subnet IDs to associate. NACLs are STATELESS — return traffic must be explicitly allowed (typically an ephemeral-port range rule 1024-65535). Rule numbers must be unique per direction within an ACL; lower numbers evaluate first.

outputs.name: firewall_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical NACL name to network ACL ID.

outputs.name: firewall_arns
outputs.description: Map of logical NACL name to network ACL ARN.

outputs.name: rule_ids
outputs.description: Map of 'ingress|egress/<acl-key>/<rule-key>' to NACL rule ID.

outputs.name: subnet_association_ids
outputs.description: Map of '<acl-key>/<subnet-id>' to NACL association ID.
